steps.title: Track the however turn
steps.content: The passage first presents the energy ladder, a common model that explains household fuel choice mainly through income. The word "however" introduces the Ethiopian study as a challenge to that model, showing that fuel use depends on several factors.

steps.title: Reject narrower purposes
steps.content: Choice A treats Egypt and Ethiopia as a simple country contrast, but Egypt is only setup. Choice B reverses the relationship by focusing on fuel use affecting income. Choice C wrongly says the model is about fuel prices. The remaining choice matches the text's purpose: challenging a common explanation for fuel choices. The answer is D.
